responses.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has exacerbated the frequency of people feeling lonely in Japan.
The isolation is unbearable for some people, leading to thoughts of suicide.
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
Prime Minister of Japan Yoshihide Suga decided to introduce the post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation.
The spread of the COVID-19 epidemic is a main factor affecting the increase in the number of suicides.
The number of suicides in Japan fell by 14% during the first wave of the pandemic in February-June 2020
Especially housewives are affected by the pandemic
Suicide rates have risen sharply in Japan in 2020.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
Psychologists believe that the problems that have arisen have become for many the last straw on the way to the decision to commit suicide.
The number of suicides has increased year on year, with particularly high rates among women and children.
The post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation was taken by Minister for Regional Development Tetsushi Sakamoto.
The psychological burden associated with the coronavirus pandemic contributed to the rise in suicide rates.
Suicide rates fell in the first half of 2020, raising hopes that the impact of the epidemic may be limited.

Islamic, Arab and other countries have united to confront the deal of the century, act on international resolutions and the Arab Peace Initiative to form an independent Palestinian state centered on East Jerusalem on the 1967 borders.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas announced that the Palestinians would no longer abide by their agreements with Israel and Washington.

The people, Islamic and national groups opposed to the Arab governments affiliated with the United States, emphasized the need for their leaders to take serious action to end the suffering of the Palestinian people and break the blockade of the Gaza Strip.
An Arab leader has invited Palestinian groups to a meeting to confront the deal of the century.
The Palestinian Authority is in favor of a two-state solution and the creation of a Palestinian state based on the 1967 borders with East Jerusalem as its capital.
Abbas reiterated his support for efforts to form unity in Palestinian positions and to confront plots that seek to deprive the Palestinian people of their legitimate rights.
Private medical and aesthetic institutions in China are mainly cosmetic surgery, but have very few doctors who truly specialize in cosmetic surgery.
Nose surgery and Botox injections are at the top of beauty operations
Some clinics around the world say the number of cosmetic surgeries has increased during the coronavirus era.
Iran is one of the top 10 countries in the world for cosmetic surgery.
Many people chose to go to Korea to do medical and aesthetic surgery in the past.
The shortage of professional doctors in China's medical beauty industry has produced many disguised solutions, such as letting consultants act as "consulting doctors".
Excessive overtaking of cosmetic surgeries has occurred even during the Coronavirus pandemic.
The demand for cosmetic surgeries in Iran is very high compared to European countries.
In South Korea, plastic surgery is as common as a haircut.
The municipal greening and cityscape administration bureau plans to build or upgrade 50 pocket parks this year.
The park did not increase the spread of COVID-19 during the pandemic, but instead provided a place of comfort for people seeking respite from social isolation.
There will be new playgrounds and sports grounds, walking paths and a large number of green spaces.
The increase in the use rate of parks is not equivalent to the increase in the transmission rate of COVID-19 in neighboring communities.
Getty Images, Saudi Arabia ’ s largest private media agency, MBC, has announced it will stop broadcasting Turkish TV series.
Turkey has contributed more than 150 TV series to an estimated 500 million viewers in 146 countries around the world.
Turkey established the Agency for Turkic Cooperation and Development to include former Soviet republics of Central Asia in its zone of influence.
Saudi Arabia has actively developed the film and television industry, and even cooperated with the United Arab Emirates in order to “fight ” with Turkey in the field of television.
Turkey is trying to draw the countries of Turkestan into its game.
Oxygen on Mars is in a related form.
NASA is developing a rocket to take people to Mars and is experimenting with producing oxygen on the planet.
This is the first time that humans have used instruments in outer space to convert other gases into oxygen.
MOXIE can obtain up to 10 grams of oxygen per hour
Such devices may one day provide astronauts with breathable air
Bayer has been accused by farmers of causing cancer by the Roundup herbicide product.
Bayer announced plans in June to settle all legal lawsuits against Monsanto for $12 billion.
The amount of compensation for each case that has been settled will be in the millions to thousands of dollars.
The International Agency for Research on Cancer considers glyphosate "probably carcinogenic"
Bayer has been sued by 52,500 plaintiffs in the United States because the herbicide glyphosate is suspected to cause cancer.
Bayer’s $10 billion legal battle settlement plan aims to end legal disputes following its acquisition of Monsanto in 2018.
The pending agreements are estimated to cover between 50,000 and 85,000 lawsuits.
The painting “Portrait of a woman ” by Gustav Klimt is worth 60 million euros.
In 1997, the police speculated that the prisoner had removed the frame from the roof of the gallery and hung the work, but the search was fruitless for many years.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.
Gardeners found the painting embedded in one of the garden walls while cleaning the gallery's garden ivy.
The painting was stolen on February 22, 1997 from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Contemporary Art as preparations for the Klimt exhibition were underway.
The picture “Portrait of a Woman ” by the Austrian artist Gustav Klimt lay in the wall for 22 years.
Upon the discovery of the painting, the thieves admitted that they had returned the artwork to the scene of the theft.
A gardener was cleaning the ivy of the museum’s outer wall when he found a metal plate on the wall, and when he opened it, he found a canvas painting in a black sackcloth bag.
The level of carbon dioxide in the Earth's atmosphere has reached a historic maximum
Rainfall in Iran is decreasing.
Drought has reduced the harvest of gardeners ’ crops in the province this year
The reduction of rainfall has led to a reduction in the province's water resources.
The Golden Bear for Best Film of the 70th Berlinale goes to "Sheytan vojud nadarad" (There Is No Evil), directed by Mohammad Rasoulof.
The film explores our responsibilities and choices in life.
Some of the refugees were Iranian.
Britain is changing the rules for accepting refugees.
British Prime Minister Boris Johnson told reporters that the most important thing is to stop refugees from coming from France, so the UK is working very closely with the French authorities.
235 illegal migrants in 17 boats attempting to cross the English Channel from northern France were intercepted by British Coast Guard and Border Guard officers on Thursday.
The English Channel is known for strong winds, high currents and heavy sea traffic, but many people are still smuggled from northern France to the United Kingdom.
Increased patrolling of beaches by French police and increased intelligence exchange between French and French law enforcement and security agencies had reduced the number of stowaways.
The authorities of the UK and France detained on Saturday 74 migrants who made an illegal attempt to cross the English Channel.
The English Channel, between France and Britain, is one of the world ’ s busiest waterways, and it can be dangerous to cross by small vessels, especially in winter when the sea is stormy.
On December 22, French police rescued 18 people attempting to cross the English Channel in two night raids, and a French official warned Britain of an increase in illegal sea migration.
Stratolaunch was tested on the runway.
The main co-founder of Stratolaunch Systems Corporation was billionaire Paul Allen.
Stratolaunch is the world’s largest aircraft.
The Roc flew at an altitude of 14,000 feet and a top speed of 320 kilometers per hour during the test flight.
The world ’ s largest aircraft has a wingspan longer than a football field.
Stratolaunch was designed to launch space rockets.
The plane was allowed to accelerate to a speed of 75 kilometers per hour during the latest tests.
Pierre-Yves Thouault, the deputy director of the Tour de France, announced that they will prosecute the woman who caused the accident.
A fan with a poster provoked a mass accident at the Tour de France
The woman was taken to the local branch of the gendarmerie
One cyclist had to be removed from the race because of the injuries
The audience was also injured in the accident.
The fall of one of the cyclists, which led to the others, provoked a fan who came too close to the roadway with a poster.
French police say an investigation will be carried out and the woman is being actively sought
Nine drivers, including German driver Jasha Suetterlin, were injured in the accident, and Jasha Suetterlin withdrew from the race and was unable to compete in the second race.
The ship docked with the International Space Station after 19 hours
SpaceX sends astronauts into space for the first time.
The return date of the Crew Dragon spacecraft may be affected by weather conditions.
Crew Dragon was launched into space with the help of a carrier rocket Falcon 9 from the Kennedy Space Center at Cape Canaveral in Florida.
A court in Ontario, Canada, ruled that Iran's actions were deliberate "terrorism" based on written evidence provided by the family.
Iran does not recognize the decisions of the Canadian court in the case of the downed Ukrainian plane
The Canadian Court does not have the jurisdiction to investigate the Ukrainian plane crash or possible failure in an incident that occurred outside the territory and jurisdiction of Canada.
Iranian authorities admitted that the Ukrainian plane was mistakenly shot down by an anti-aircraft missile system of air defense
Iran is prepared to pay compensation to the families of the victims of Ukraine International Airlines Flight PS752, which was shot down near Tehran in January.
Iran has already announced its official protest to the Canadian government "through the relevant channels" and reserves the right to "pursue political and legal measures in this regard".
Coordination is currently underway for negotiations on the amount of compensation by the Islamic Republic of Iran to the victims of the incident, the Ukrainian airline and the aircraft manufacturers.
The Russian embassy in Pyongyang said there were 17 crew members on board the detained vessel, of whom 15 were Russian citizens and two were South Koreans.
Russia calls on North Korea to release detained ship
North Korean border guards detained the Russian fishing vessel “Xianghailin-8” on the night of July 17
The ship was detained for violating North Korean entry rules
The international community has condemned Japan's decision to restart commercial whaling
Japan is resuming commercial whaling for the first time in 37 years.
Whaling is part of the Japanese tradition.
Last year, Japanese whale-hunting vessels caught at least 333 minke whales, or small fin whales, for what they described as "scientific research".
Japan has decided to withdraw from the International Whaling Commission (IWC).
The Japan Fisheries Agency set a limit of 227 for whaling up to the end of December this year, including 52 minke whales, 150 Bryde whales and 25 Sei whales.
Hemingway's dialogues had a magical influence on many writers
Ernest Hemingway wrote from real, live experiences.
This project is a collaboration between China, the European Union, India, Japan, South Korea, Russia and the United States.

The fully superconducting tokamak nuclear fusion experimental device (EAST) of the Hefei Institute of Materials of the Chinese Academy of Sciences has successfully achieved repeatable 120 million degrees centigrade and 101 seconds of plasma operation.
Nuclear fusion provides clean, reliable and carbon-free energy
China, India, Japan, Korea, Russia and the United States participated in and contributed to the ITER project with the European Union.
In 2019, the process of creating a Chinese experimental reactor for thermonuclear fusion EAST will be completed
Nuclear fusion fuel, deuterium and tritium, can be extracted from seawater
